module_goal: Fragrance, texture, lather, post-wash feel, and packaging. The #1 driver in body wash choice.

questions.question_text: What fragrance families do you naturally gravitate toward? Pick your top 2:

options.label: Fresh/Citrus — lemon, orange, grapefruit
Floral — rose, jasmine, lavender, mogra
Woody/Musky — sandalwood, cedarwood, warm temple smell
Herbal/Earthy — neem, tulsi, aloe vera, wet earth after rain
Sweet/Gourmand — vanilla, chocolate, caramel, coffee
Ocean/Aquatic — sea breeze, fresh rain
Spicy/Warm — cinnamon, clove, black pepper
Fragrance-free — I prefer no noticeable scent

questions.question_text: Name a specific product whose fragrance you loved. Did you repurchase it because of the scent? What made that fragrance special to you?
questions.placeholder: This can be any product — body wash, perfume, shampoo, candle, anything

questions.question_text: How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin?

options.label: I don't want it to linger — fresh during shower is enough
1-2 hours — a light scent that fades naturally
Half the day — noticeable till afternoon
All day — I want people to notice it

questions.question_text: How important is lather/foam to your body wash experience?

options.label: Essential — more foam = feels cleaner
Nice to have — I like some foam but not a dealbreaker
Don't care — I don't notice lather
Prefer low-lather — I like a creamy, lotion-like feel

questions.question_text: What texture do you prefer in a body wash?

options.label: Thick creamy gel — like a rich lotion (e.g., Dove)
Lightweight liquid — thin, watery, spreads easily (e.g., Dettol)
Mousse or foam — fluffy foam from a pump
Oil-to-foam — starts as oil, turns foamy with water
Exfoliating with beads or scrub particles

questions.question_text: What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling?

options.label: Squeaky clean — tight, all oil removed
Moisturized and soft — smooth, hydrated
Fresh and tingling — cool/minty sensation
Neutral — no residue, just clean
Silky smooth — velvety soft, almost slippery

questions.question_text: Do you prefer packaging that looks:

options.label: Clinical/medical — white, clean (think Cetaphil, Minimalist)
Luxurious/premium — metallic accents, elegant (think Bath & Body Works)
Natural/earthy — brown, green, recycled look (think Forest Essentials)
Playful/colorful — bright, fun (think Fiama, mCaffeine)
Minimalist/clean — simple, modern (think Muji, Plum)

questions.question_text: If you could combine the fragrance of one product with the texture of another, what would your ideal combination be?
